title: Perplexity AI for Professional Research

modules.title: What Perplexity Actually Does: Search, Synthesis, and Where It Differs from Google
modules.sub: Understanding the engine beneath the answers Perplexity returns
modules.description: Learners examine how Perplexity retrieves and synthesizes information differently from traditional search engines, mapping the mechanics behind its cited responses. They compare real queries run in both Perplexity and Google to surface concrete differences in result structure, sourcing, and reliability. By the end, they can articulate what Perplexity is actually doing under the hood and why that distinction matters for professional use.

modules.title: Prompt Strategies That Get Research-Grade Answers, Not Summaries
modules.sub: Crafting queries that pull precise intelligence, not surface overviews
modules.description: Learners practice constructing targeted prompts using techniques such as scope constraints, role framing, and follow-up chaining to push Perplexity beyond generic summaries. They test multiple prompt variations on the same research question and compare the depth and specificity of the outputs produced. The module concludes with learners building a personal prompt template they can immediately apply to their own professional research contexts.

modules.title: Evaluating Source Quality and Spotting Hallucination in Cited Responses
modules.sub: Auditing Perplexity citations before trusting what it tells you
modules.description: Learners develop a systematic method for checking whether Perplexity's cited sources actually support the claims made in its responses, identifying common hallucination patterns. They work through annotated examples where citations are misleading, missing, or misrepresented, practicing the habit of tracing every consequential claim back to its source. By the module's end, learners can apply a rapid credibility audit to any Perplexity output before acting on it professionally.

modules.title: Integrating Perplexity into Daily Professional Research Workflows
modules.sub: Fitting Perplexity into real work without disrupting existing processes
modules.description: Learners map their current research workflows and identify specific friction points where Perplexity can accelerate background scanning, literature review, or competitive monitoring. They design a lightweight integration plan that connects Perplexity to existing tools and establishes clear handoff points where human verification takes over. The module produces a concrete, role-specific workflow diagram the learner can put into practice immediately.

modules.title: When Not to Use Perplexity: Knowing the Tool's Hard Limits
modules.sub: Recognising the research tasks where Perplexity reliably fails you
modules.description: Learners examine categories of research where Perplexity's architecture introduces unacceptable risk, including real-time data needs, primary source requirements, and high-stakes factual accuracy scenarios. They apply a decision framework to a set of realistic professional research tasks, sorting them into Perplexity-appropriate and Perplexity-inappropriate buckets with clear reasoning. The module closes with learners drafting personal guardrails that define when they will and will not reach for Perplexity in their own work.

synopsis: Perplexity AI sits at the intersection of search and language generation â and knowing how to use it deliberately separates professionals who get actionable intelligence from those who get plausible-sounding noise. This focused course teaches working professionals how to query effectively, assess citations critically, and embed Perplexity into research workflows without sacrificing accuracy for speed.
rationale: Perplexity is rapidly replacing traditional search for professional research tasks, yet most users apply it with no more rigor than a Google search â leading to confidently wrong outputs in high-stakes decisions.
learning_outcome: Learners will be able to construct targeted Perplexity queries, critically evaluate the sourced responses it generates, and make an informed decision about when it outperforms or should be replaced by traditional search methods.
